We are a medium-sized group practice designated as an Essential Service Provider.

name: Cairn Counseling Denver
description:
Cairn Counseling specializes in treating individuals who have experienced trauma to reduce the negative impact on their livelihoods. Our Clinicians have experience working with many individuals, including couples, families, children, teens, adults, people of color (POC), and lesbian, bisexual, transgender, and queer folks. We at Cairn Counseling believe that everyone deserves to experience effective and affirming therapy. Using eclectic therapy techniques, our skillful and compassionate therapists walk with clients through the hardships of their lives. All individuals are welcome.

Our therapists use a variety of methods, suited to the individual, to treat trauma, substance use disorder, and work through life-changes. Some of these methods include: Solution Focused Brief Therapy (SFBT), Trauma Focused Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Narrative Therapy, Play Therapy, Acceptance and Commitment Therapy, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR), Somatic Therapy, and more.

While they will be provided with comprehensive training initially and throughout their placement, interns at Cairn are expected to function as therapists, meaning they regularly meet with clients, bill for sessions, and complete comprehensive documentation–including meeting Medicaid compliance standards. The internship very closely resembles a therapy career within a group practice and provides useful preparation for entry into the mental health field post-graduation.

All applicants for a Cairn Counseling internship must:

- Be currently enrolled in a master’s or doctoral program
- Attend one-hour of individual supervision per week
- Attend one-hour of group supervision every two weeks
- Have ability to work independently
- Have mission alignment
- Possess self-awareness and a commitment to personal growth.
- Attend onboarding training for the first two weeks of placement (preparing for a - Mon-Friday, 9-5 schedule in those first few weeks)
- Have ability to schedule clients at least one evening (4-7pm)
- Have ability to respond to client emergencies, such as mandatory reports, as needed (typically occurs infrequently)
- Complete all clinical and practice paperwork & billing within the required timelines
languages: No additional language requirements or preferences
deadline: April 24th 2026
applyMethod:
Complete an online application or submit documents via a portal:

https://www.cairncounselingdenver.com/careers

name: Center for Resilience Strategies
description:
At Center for Resilience Strategies, we are dedicated to offering professional mentorship to graduate students seeking their degree in counseling, social work, or marriage and family therapy. By teaching our innovative practices, interns are given the opportunity to develop their clinical skills with the ongoing support of licensed therapists. We are committed to the growth and development of early career therapists, seek to make a positive impact on the mental health field, and we aim to increase the accessibility and affordability of mental health care.

We are a group practice based in the Denver metro area that aims to provide accessible, affordable, and collaborative outpatient mental health care to individual adults, children, families, and couples. CRS has office locations in Denver, Wheat Ridge, Westminster, Aurora, Arvada, and Lakewood CO. Our licensed therapists are credentialed with Medicaid and most major insurance providers, and we are able to provide supervisory billing for some insurance plans. Interns are also able to offer affordable, out-of-pocket rates through a sliding scale option. We provide onboarding training and orientation for new interns as well as weekly individual supervision and group supervision offerings for all student trainees, led by licensed clinicians.

Aurora: 10800 E Bethany Drive, Suite 100 Aurora, CO 80014
Lakewood: 7175 W Jefferson Ave, Suite 1300 Lakewood, CO 80235
Wheat Ridge: 6990 W. 38th Ave. Suite 104 Wheat Ridge, CO 80033

Please also complete the online application via our website: https://www.crscounseling.com/internships/

After reviewing the application materials, we will set up a time for a virtual interview. We may set up additional interviews as needed to ensure proper fit with our practice and internship program. Before providing an official offer, applicants will be provided a link to complete a reference check.
contact: For any questions or additional info, please reach out to the Internship Director, Ben Lawful, at ben@crscounseling.com

name: Center for Valued Living*
description: At the Center for Valued Living, we stand firmly against all forms of discrimination and injustice. Our commitment to anti-racism and queer-allyship, and our unwavering support for marginalized communities are resolute. We are dedicated to fostering a safe, inclusive space where every individual is valued, heard, and empowered. Our advocacy for equality, social justice, and mental health equity is unshakeable, and we will continue to stand with those who need it most. If you’re looking for mental health providers that recognize the complexities of your experience and are committed to supporting you with care and respect, we invite you to reach out. Together, we can work toward healing and growth.

name: Children's Wellness Center of Colorado, PLLC*
description: We are a private psychotherapy practice that serves children, adolescents, their parents/caregivers, young adults, adults without children, couples and families. Many of our clients have experienced trauma. We help students develop skills in trauma focused care as well as skills to work with clients of all ages, starting as young as 3 years old. We highly recommend that students complete Synergetic Play Therapy training during their internship to gain the necessary skills to work with younger children. We provide weekly individual supervision as well as group supervision once per month and administrative meetings which include case consultation twice per month. Our team is warm, inviting and invested in supporting each other. In addition to direct therapy hours, you'll learn how to screen new clients, complete case management, coordinate care with outside entities and complete insurance compliant documentation. We seek highly motivated and engaged students who would like to provide high-quality therapeutic services as well as engage in systemic collaboration.

name: Colorado Dream Foundation- Beyond Dreams Therapy
description:
Overview
The Clinical Intern is a key member of the Beyond Dreams Therapy team at Colorado Dream Foundation (CDF). This position supports the behavioral and mental health needs of clients ages four through adulthood. Working under licensed supervision, the intern provides therapeutic services to clients and contributes to program facilitation, clinical care, and community engagement.
This role is ideal for graduate students in their concentration year (social work) or practicum/internship phase (counseling) who are seeking hands-on clinical experience in a community-based setting.
Upon successful completion of their internship, Master’s-level candidates will be invited to interview for the Clinical Fellowship Program. Selected fellows will transition into an Associate Behavioral Health Therapist role, where they will maintain and grow their caseload, receive a minimum of eleven months of clinical supervision toward licensure, and receive an hourly pay increase aligned with their advanced training and experience.
Key Responsibilities: 
Clinical Service Delivery
Provide direct therapeutic care to clients ages four through adulthood, including assessment, treatment planning, and ongoing support.
Maintain a caseload of 10–12 clients and provide consistent, high-quality care aligned with trauma-informed and healing-centered practices.
Develop and implement individualized treatment plans that promote clients’ emotional, behavioral, and social well-being.
Facilitate or co-facilitate educational and therapeutic groups for clients and community members as assigned.
Collaboration and Supervision
Participate in weekly individual supervision with a licensed clinician (LPC, LCSW, or LSW) to support clinical skill development and ensure high standards of care.
Attend and contribute to group supervision sessions with other interns.
Collaborate with multidisciplinary teams, including youth program team members and external partners, to ensure coordinated support for youth and families.
Data, Documentation, and Compliance
Conduct behavioral health assessments to inform treatment planning and crisis intervention.
Maintain accurate, timely case notes, reports, and documentation in compliance with state, federal, and organizational standards.
Participate in quality assurance processes and support data collection to strengthen program evaluation and reporting.
Community Engagement and Representation
Serve as an advocate for youth and families within the CDF network and the broader community.
Build strong, trusting relationships with clients, caregivers, and staff that support long-term growth and resilience.
Participate in CDF all-staff training, meetings, and events, contributing to a culture of collaboration and continuous learning.
compensationNote: Yes - stipend for all hours worked.
languages: Preferred: Spanish
deadline: All applications need to be submitted by February 28th
applyMethod:

All applications can be submitted through our website. The link will go live at the end of January 

https://beyonddreamstherapy.coloradodream.org/internship-program
contact:
Sophie Silver- Manager of Therapeutic Services 
ssilver@coloradodream.org

name: Denver Children's Advocacy Center*
description: Our mission is to prevent abuse, strengthen families, and restore childhood. We have three primary programs at DCAC working towards our mission: Rapid Response (Forensic Interviewers, Victim Advocates), Treatment and Assessment, and Community Outreach. As a children's advocacy center we partner closely with our Multi-Disciplinary Team partners (Denver Police, Department of Human Services, Denver Health, Denver District Attorney) to provide a child-centered investigative process when there is an allegation of abuse or neglect. Our treatment team provides evidence-based, trauma-informed services to children and adolescents who have experienced trauma, and our community outreach team partners with the community to provide resources and prevention education.

Our internship program is largely focused on individual and group psychotherapy with adults. Interns also learn the process of completing clinical diagnostic intakes with clients of all ages.

name: Geek Therapeutics*
description:
At Geek Therapeutics, our mission is to innovate mental health services and education by integrating evidence-based therapeutic models with the rich, immersive worlds of geek culture. We provide neuro affirming therapy services that use storytelling, gaming, and fandoms to foster emotional healing, personal growth, and community connection. Simultaneously, we advance professional development through evidence-based continuing education programs, empowering clinicians and educators to incorporate therapeutic gaming and geek-inspired interventions into their practice. Our goal is to inspire both individual transformation and professional excellence, creating meaningful change through the unique lens of geek culture.
 
 We believe therapy should reflect the identities, interests, and lived experiences of our clients. That’s why our services are built on a foundation of:
 ● Evidence-Based Modalities: Including CBT, DBT, ACT, EMDR, and narrative therapy.
 ● Culturally Competent Care: Tailored support for marginalized, neurodivergent, LGBTQIA+, veteran, and geek-identifying clients.
 ● Innovative Interventions: Integrating pop culture, tabletop role-playing games (TTRPGs), video games, storytelling, and fandom to increase engagement and insight. Our goal is to help clients “reforge” their identity, wellness, and connection with self and others by meeting them in the world they feel most seen and empowered. 
 
 Clinical Services Offered
 ● Individual Therapy for children, teens, and adults
 ● Family and Couples Therapy
 ● Group Therapy focused on anxiety, social skills, identity development, grief, and more
 ● TTRPG-Facilitated Therapy groups led by Certified Geek Therapists and Therapeutic Game Masters
 ● Psychological Assessment & Testing (available in select locations)
 ● Telehealth Services offered across multiple states
languages: No additional language requirements or preferences

name: Greenlake Psychology
description:
Greenlake Psychology is a small private practice in East Denver. We use a family systems and psychodynamic approach to therapy with our clients. We are a generalist practice and serve children, adults, couples, and families. Our main areas of focus include complex family systems, child behavior and parenting concerns, neurodivergence, and Jewish clients seeking culturally-affirming care.

Do you want to know the ins and outs of running a successful private practice? Are you looking for strong generalist training from attentive, experienced, and supportive supervisors?

At Greenlake Psychology, we invest in our interns. With over 15 years of clinical, teaching, and supervision experience, we provide a comprehensive, challenging, and enjoyable internship year that will prepare you for your future career. We understand the unique needs of interns and structure the internship year developmentally to help you get the most from this experience.

We are seeking independent, kindhearted interns who love a challenge and who value the relationships in their life. You will be part of a two-person cohort of interns and will work closely with Dr. Rachel Gall and Dr. Keegan Walker as part of your training.

We prefer to take on practicum students who would like to stay on for their internship year.

Professional counseling, marriage and family therapy, and psychology interns may apply.

Opportunities include:
Child, adult, couples, and family therapy
One hour of individual supervision per week
One hour of group supervision per week
One seminar per week related to current issues in psychotherapy
$300 stipend for additional training, conferences, or therapy materials
Administrative, bookkeeping, and marketing experience
Inclusion in administrative decisions and meetings
Flexible work schedule
Co-therapy with supervisor
addresses: In East Denver just south of Lowry
compensationNote: Other: $300 professonal development stipend (for trainings, materials, etc.)

name: Interfaith Bridge Counseling, PLLC
description:
Interfaith Bridge Counseling, PLLC is a mental health organization that offers accessible, quality individual counseling, teen groups, and community workshops to strengthen the communities of our beloved Colorado. It is our mission to provide Colorado with a brave and safer community of therapist(s) and educators serving tweens, teens, and twenty-somethings of all kinds in their personal growth, community healing, and multicultural values.

Here at Interfaith Bridge Counseling, we are intentional about hiring Interns who align with both our mission and our values as a means to foster a more equitable and communal mental health community. Every member of our mental health community, including staff members, therapists, and clients, embody our mission as well as our core values of Accessibility, Community, Advocacy, Belonging, and Collective Liberation.

name: Knowles Consulting and Psychological Services*
description:
Knowles Consulting and Psychological Services (KCAPS) is a culturally-centered, strengths-based, and trauma-informed practice dedicated to serving the diverse needs of the community. Founded by Odessia Knowles, PsyD, a licensed psychologist in the state of Colorado, the practice is grounded in a multicultural feminist approach that values equity, collaboration, and empowerment. KCAPS provides services to individuals of all ages, as well as couples and families, recognizing that healing often happens both individually and within relationships. The team works collaboratively with clients, colleagues, and community partners to provide thoughtful, responsive care. The practice serves a wide range of diverse and complex cases, meeting individuals and families where they are with compassion and clinical excellence. At KCAPS, clinicians strive to be more than just therapists—they aim to be advocates, collaborators, and consistent supports within the communities they serve.
 At KCAPS, we seek students who are ready to step into a space that fosters growth—not only in cultural awareness, but also in clinical skill development. Our caseload extends beyond “cookie-cutter” presentations of anxiety and depression, offering exposure to diverse, complex, and meaningful work. We value supporting students as they explore their professional interests, deepen their clinical identity, and discover potential niches along the way.

name: Momentum Counseling and Consulting
description:
Our mission at Momentum Counseling and Consulting is to create a client-centric experience that inspires hope, promotes wellness, and empowers individuals and families through compassionate, accessible, and evidence-based mental health care. We are dedicated to delivering ethical, effective, and empathetic services, providing clients with opportunities to embrace their strengths, navigate life’s challenges with purpose, and achieve lasting emotional well-being within a supportive and inclusive community.

Clients we serve: Individuals, couples, and families of all ages whether it is in-person, hybrid, and remote counseling.

Areas we treat: Addictions, adolescents, young adults, anxiety, depression, autism, ADHD, chronic illness, couples counseling (including non-traditional relationships), eating disorders, family therapy, grief, relationships, attachment issues, trauma, men’s issues, OCD, perfectionism, performance anxiety, trauma, career, self-esteem, body image, LGBTQIA+ community members, trans-youth.

Modalities we use: Our therapists are trained in a variety of modalities including: Gottman Method, Cognitive Behavioral Therapy, Client-Centered Therapy, Mindfulness-Based Counseling, Systemic Therapy, Narrative Therapy, Somatic Therapy, Internal Family Systems, Dialectic Behavior Therapy, and Emotionally-Focused Couples Therapy.

name: Monarch Mental Health
description:
We are a residential clinical mental health program for clients with severe and persistent mental illness. Most of our clients come from state hospital's and psychiatric facilities for placement often engaging in community connections and support for the first time in many years. 
Our mission is to provide comprehensive, individualized mental health treatment across multiple levels of care, reducing barriers to mental health stability. Through the integration of psychiatry, primary clinical treatment, primary care physical health and holistic wellness practices, we support each person’s unique path toward stability, recovery, and meaningful connection. We are committed to meeting individuals where they are, delivering evidence-based care with dignity, collaboration, and respect.
languages: No additional language requirements or preferences
deadline: We accept applications on a rolling basis (no specific deadlines)
applyMethod:
Email application materials to:

Chris Dow
cdow@monarchmentalhealth.org

name: Mountain Thrive Counseling, PLLC
description: Mountain Thrive Counseling, PLLC is a compassionate, equity-driven outpatient group therapy practice serving children, adults, and families through in-person services in Denver and telehealth therapy across Colorado. Our team is deeply committed to anti-racism, social justice, and culturally congruent care. We work with a diverse client population experiencing a wide range of mental health needs, including anxiety, depression, trauma, serious mental illness, life transitions, LGBTQ+ affirming needs and more. Please consider joining us to learn from a supportive team.
populations: depression or other behavioral health needs.
People who are trans or part of the LGBTQ+ communities.

description: We provide individual therapy, couples therapy and neurofeedback therapy. We primarily focus on treating trauma, anxiety/depression as well as support for individuals on the Autism Spectrum. We provide collaborative and supportive support to help manage mental health symptoms.

name: North Range Behavioral Health
description:
At North Range Behavioral Health, we believe no one should face mental health challenges alone. Our compassionate team provides comprehensive, person-centered care, supporting individuals, families, and communities to thrive. We’re proud to be a leader in behavioral health services across Weld County, offering a wide range of programs for all ages and backgrounds.

We focus on collaboration and trauma-informed care, integrating clinical expertise with research to create meaningful, lasting impact. Join us in making a difference!

Vision: Where Hope Begins and Change Is Possible

Mission: Compassionate care for those facing mental health and addiction challenges

Values:

Customer First: Supporting recovery every day
Compassion: Treating all with empathy and respect
Collaboration: Building strong relationships for effective care
addresses: We have locations in Greeley, Windsor, Frederick and Fort Lupton. Locations may vary each time we post internships.
languages: No additional language requirements or preferences
deadline: Application will be open February 15-May 15.
applyMethod:
Complete an online application or submit documents via a portal:

Go to www.northrange.org and select Careers then search open positions, once you get to that screen filter by position type and select internship.
contact: Courtney Schaper, Human Resources Specialist. courtney.schaper@northrange.org

name: Servicios de la Raza*
description:
Driven by the strength of community and committed to service, the focus is on creating meaningful impact. Every effort is centered around empowering individuals, fostering growth, and supporting well-being. Through dedication and a shared vision, the work is not just a service—it’s a commitment to making a difference in the lives of those served.
Servicios offers a range of internship opportunities designed to provide hands-on experience in impactful fields. Internships are available in social work, human services, criminal justice, and behavioral health, all within a supportive environment that encourages both personal and professional development.
languages: Preferred: Spanish

do the work. Please do not use online stock items. Get creative and channel your inner …  and please include a detailed explanation of the piece you chose
as this will help us understand your motivation and suitability for the placement.

name: Stein Counseling
description: We are a group of professional counselors who provide collaborative, quality mental health care. We provide individual therapy for all ages, in addition to couples and family therapy. Within our site, we see a range of mental health disorders, races, SES, genders, and sexual orientations. Our mission is to provide affordable, easily accessible care to all.
languages: No additional language requirements or preferences

name: Tennyson Center for Children
description:
Tennyson Center for Children has served Colorado’s most neglected, abused, and traumatized children since 1904. We are known for our high standard of care across all our programs and services. These services include an accredited, Therapeutic K-12 School and Day Treatment Program with a 4:1 student-to-staff ratio on our campus and in-home and Community Programs in 12 counties across the state to provide preventive services and therapy to strengthen families with children aged 0 to adulthood.

At Tennyson, our goal is to keep families strong and keep them together. We provide an array of community services that aims to do exactly that by creating access to therapy, skills building, and resource connection. We offer support and therapy to kids and families across Colorado – and beyond.
languages: No additional language requirements or preferences

name: Trident Therapy Center
description: We offer effective outpatient, individualized, and group psychological care.  Our client centered practice works with clients and their care providers to achieve desired goals.  Counseling, coaching, hypnotherapy, and pain management are efficient therapeutic tools for physical, behavioral, and emotional states to enhance mental health, well-being, provide emotional transformation and increase the healing process more rapidly.  We also work with court-ordered DUI/DWAI clients in group and individual sessions.

name: VitalCare
description: VitalCare seeks to bridge the gap between private practice and community mental health by offering consistent, high-quality mental health care to clients of all socioeconomic backgrounds. We provide individual therapy for clients ages 4 through end of life. We offer a variety of modalities, including trauma-informed talk therapy, EMDR, grief counseling, and somatic therapy. We have the resources to allow art, play, movement, and sand trays to be used in sessions. We provide in person and virtual therapy for our community.

description: We focus on training and taking care of our therapists so that we are socially justice minded and focused on creating accessibility to our clients. We complete individual and group psychotherapy for individuals with a variety of insurances and support our trainees with supervision, trainings, and a whole identity group. Our focus for training is centered on creating a holding environment for people to grow and learn in.

description: Wounded Healer Project is a veteran mental health nonprofit in which all of our counselors are veterans themselves. Our mission is to prevent veteran suicide, empower veteran mental health providers, and serve marginalized veterans and their families.
